name: Gajar ka Halwa
tagline: Slow-cooked carrot dessert with milk and ghee — vitamin A and calcium
highlight: Cook the grated carrot in milk first — the milk reduces and clings to every shred

hero.alt: Orange-red carrot halwa garnished with slivered almonds and pistachios in a brass katori
hero.caption: gajar ka halwa — winter pudding, slow-reduced

healthFacts: Red carrots are loaded with beta-carotene — converts to vitamin A for vision and skin.
Milk concentration adds calcium, B2, and B12.
Ghee and nuts deliver vitamin E and healthy mono-unsaturated fats.
Best made in winter when red Delhi carrots are sweet — natural sugar reduces.

steps.title: Reduce in milk
steps.detail: Combine grated carrot and milk in a heavy pan. Bring to a boil, then simmer on medium-low for 30–35 minutes, stirring every 4 minutes, till the milk is fully absorbed.
steps.tip: This step cannot be hurried — the long reduction is what makes halwa taste like halwa.

steps.title: Add sugar and ghee
steps.detail: Once the milk is gone, add sugar and stir 5 minutes — sugar releases more water that cooks off. Add ghee a tablespoon at a time, stirring till the halwa stops sticking to the pan.
steps.tip: Ghee at the end gives gloss; ghee at the start gets absorbed and disappears.

steps.title: Finish with khoya and nuts
steps.detail: Stir in crumbled khoya (if using) and cardamom. Cook 3 more minutes till thick and glossy. Garnish with slivered almonds and pistachios. Serve warm.
steps.tip: Tastes even better the next day — cardamom needs time to settle.
